docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Raspberry Pi–based septic monitoring system using GPIO inputs and Node-RED for state processing.

docs.title: What This Does
docs.text:
• Reads float switches and pump states
  • Normalizes and interprets signals in Node-RED
  • Publishes system state locally and remotely

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. GPIO state is captured and written to /run/gpio_snapshot.json
  2. Node-RED processes the data
  3. System state is encoded as a bitmask
  4. Data is published to MQTT, dashboards, and external services

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
Use the navigation menu to explore:

  • System → architecture and data flow
  • Services → scripts and systemd services
  • Node-RED → processing logic and state handling
  • Interfaces → GPIO, MQTT, and file structures
  • Diagrams → visual system layout

docs.title: Status
docs.text: System is operational and documentation is in progress.

docs.title: Digital Path
docs.text: GPIO → chkgpio.py → MQTT → Node-RED → InfluxDB → Grafana

docs.title: Snapshot Path
docs.text: GPIO → gpio-heartbeat.sh → /run/gpio_snapshot.json → (consumer TBD)

docs.title: Digital State Data Flow
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[GPIO Pins Hardware] --> B[gpio_heartbeat.sh]
    B --> C[gpio_snapshot.json - raw]
    C --> D[Node-RED]

    D --> E[Normalize + Inversion]
    D --> F[Bitmask Construction]
    D --> G[RBE change detection]

    G --> H[Local MQTT system/state]
    G --> I[Adafruit IO]
    G --> J[External systems AIQSO]

docs.title: Analog Path
docs.text: Level Sensor (4–20mA) → ADS1115 → level-gem2.py → MQTT → Node-RED → InfluxDB → Grafana

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Raspberry Pi–based septic monitoring appliance.

Monitors: - Digital inputs (floats, pumps, alarms) - Analog level sensor (4–20 mA via ADS1115)

Publishes: - MQTT (events + level) - Local files (logs + snapshots)

docs.title: gpio-heartbeat.service
docs.text:
• Periodic GPIO snapshot (5s)
  • Output: /run/gpio_snapshot.json

docs.title: chkgpio.service
docs.text:
• GPIO change detection (50ms polling)
  • Outputs:
  • MQTT events
  • local log file

docs.title: level-gem.service
docs.text:
• Analog level measurement (1s)
  • Output:
  • MQTT JSON

docs.title: MQTT
docs.text:
• Digital events (per GPIO)
  • System status
  • Analog level data

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• Snapshot: /run/gpio_snapshot.json
  • Event log: /opt/poopi/app/gpio_events.log

docs.title: Node-RED Role
docs.text:
Node-RED acts as the central processing engine:

  • Converts GPIO snapshots into system state
  • Processes analog level data
  • Performs smoothing and trend analysis
  • Routes data to multiple MQTT brokers
  • Provides dashboard visualization
  • Implements watchdog recovery logic

docs.title: System Diagram
docs.text:
graph TD
  GPIO --> chkgpio
  GPIO --> heartbeat
  chkgpio --> MQTT
  heartbeat --> JSON
  LevelSensor --> ADS1115 --> levelgem
  levelgem --> MQTT

docs.title: Snapshot
docs.text: /run/gpio_snapshot.json - full state every 5s

docs.title: Event Log
docs.text: /opt/poopi/app/gpio_events.log - append-only - per-event logging

docs.title: GPIO Map
docs.text: Pin Label Inverted 17 surge-pump-cycle No 22 surge-ops-float No 5 surge-override-float No 27 surge-hwa No 26 effluent-pump-cycle No 20 effluent-ops-float Yes 19 effluent-override-float No 21 effluent-hwa No

docs.title: Digital (GPIO Events)
docs.text: zmcnair/feeds/bkh. - payload: 0 or 1

docs.title: Status
docs.text: zmcnair/feeds/bkh.chkgpio-status - payload: - 1 = online - 0 = offline

docs.title: Analog (Level Sensor)
docs.text:
sensors/septic/level - payload (JSON):

```json { "volts": float, "ma": float, "above_sensor_in": float, "total_depth_in": float }

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Convert raw GPIO snapshot data into a structured system state and distribute it via MQTT.

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
Source: /run/gpio_snapshot.json

  • Updated every 5 seconds by gpio-heartbeat
  • Contains full GPIO state snapshot

docs.title: 1. Parse JSON
docs.text: Extract: - gpio17 - gpio22 - gpio5 - etc.

docs.title: 2. Build System State Bitmask
docs.text:
Each input is mapped to a bit position:

Bit Signal 7 surge pump 6 surge high water 5 surge ops float 4 surge override 3 effluent pump 2 effluent high water 1 effluent ops float 0 effluent override

Outputs: - Decimal value - Binary string - Hex representation

docs.title: 3. Change Detection (RBE)
docs.text:
• Prevents duplicate messages
  • Only forwards when state changes

docs.title: MQTT (Local)
docs.text:
Topic: system/state

Payload: - Integer bitmask - Each bit represents a specific float or pump state

docs.title: Inversion Strategy
docs.text: GPIO values are read as raw electrical states from: /run/gpio_snapshot.json These values are not modified by upstream scripts.

docs.title: Logical Interpretation
docs.text:
Some inputs are wired as active-low and require inversion to reflect system meaning.

Inversion is applied in Node-RED prior to bitmask construction.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Signal GPIO Raw Logical Effluent Ops Float GPIO20 1 0

docs.title: Bitmask Mapping
docs.text:
The system state is encoded as an 8-bit mask where each bit represents a GPIO input.

Bit Weight GPIO Signal 7 128 17 Surge Pump 6 64 27 Surge High Water 5 32 22 Surge Ops Float 4 16 5 Surge Override 3 8 26 Effluent Pump 2 4 21 Effluent High Water 1 2 20 Effluent Ops Float 0 1 19 Effluent Override

docs.title: Bitmask Calculation
docs.text: Each active signal contributes its weight to the total mask value.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Bit positions are fixed and must match Node-RED implementation
  • Interpretation depends on correct pin polarity (inversion settings)
  • This table should be updated as system behavior is validated
  • Raw values represent electrical state only and should not be used directly for interpretation
  • Logical values represent system behavior
  • All system state calculations use logical (post-inversion) values
  • System behavior should be interpreted using combinations of signals rather than individual bits

docs.title: Local
docs.text: • localhost (Aedes)

docs.title: Adafruit IO
docs.text: • io.adafruit.com

docs.title: AIQSO
docs.text: • ws://mqtt.aiqso.net

docs.title: Role
docs.text:
Node-RED acts as the central processing and routing layer for:

  • GPIO state (via snapshot file)
  • Analog level data (via MQTT)
  • System telemetry
  • External MQTT distribution

docs.title: Core Functions
docs.text:
1. Read GPIO snapshot file
  2. Build system state (bitmask)
  3. Process level sensor data
  4. Perform smoothing and trend analysis
  5. Route data to multiple MQTT endpoints
  6. Provide dashboard visualization
  7. Monitor system health (watchdog)

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text: Source Method GPIO snapshot file read (/run/gpio_snapshot.json) Level sensor MQTT (sensors/septic/level) CPU temp local command Enclosure temp AHT20 sensor

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text:
• MQTT (multiple brokers)
  • Dashboard (UI)
  • Debug/logging

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Process analog level sensor data into usable values, trends, and publish them via MQTT.

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
MQTT Topic: sensors/septic/level

Payload (JSON): ```json { "volts": float, "ma": float, "above_sensor_in": float, "total_depth_in": float }

docs.title: 1. Smoothing
docs.text:
• Moving average (~5 samples)
  • Reduces noise and jitter

docs.title: 2. Unit Conversion
docs.text:
Constant: - ~42.56 gallons per inch

Derived: - gallons = inches × constant

docs.title: 3. Derived Values
docs.text:
• Total depth (inches)
  • Volume (gallons)
  • Fill level (relative)

docs.title: 4. Trend Analysis
docs.text:
Short-Term (~minutes): - Detect pump activity - Rapid changes

Long-Term (~hours): - Detect filling/draining behavior

Outputs: - rising - falling - steady - rate (gallons/hour)

docs.title: MQTT (Local)
docs.text:
Topic: surge/tank/gallons

Payload: - Float (gallons)

docs.title: MQTT (External)
docs.text: Adafruit IO Topics: zmcnair/feeds/bkh.surge-level zmcnair/feeds/bkh.surge-inches

docs.title: Level Interpretation (Work in Progress)
docs.text: Gallons Inches Condition TBD TBD Normal idle level TBD TBD Pump active (rapid drop) TBD TBD Rising level (inflow) TBD TBD High level / alert condition

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Values are smoothed before publishing
  • External publishing is rate-limited
  • Calibration depends on sensor accuracy and constants

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Detect loss of telemetry and recover automatically.

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
Monitored Topic: zmcnair/feeds/sensors.burlesonkh-septic-cpu

Purpose: - Confirms system is alive and publishing data - Used as heartbeat signal

docs.title: 1. Track Last Update
docs.text: • Store timestamp of last received message

docs.title: 2. Timeout Detection
docs.text: Trigger if: - No data received for ~180 seconds

docs.title: 3. Failure Counter
docs.text:
• Increment on each missed interval
  • Reset when data resumes

docs.title: 4. Escalation Logic
docs.text: Conditions: - Multiple consecutive failures - System uptime exceeds threshold - Cooldown period has elapsed

docs.title: Action
docs.text: Command: ```bash sudo systemctl restart NetworkManager

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text:
MQTT Topic: - zmcnair/feeds/bkh.bkh-watchdog

Payload - Status or alert message indicating watchdog activity

docs.title: Watchdog Interpretation (Work in Progress)
docs.text: State Meaning Normal Telemetry received regularly Timeout No data within expected interval Recovering Restart action triggered Recovered Data flow restored

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Prevents system lockups due to network failure
  • Includes cooldown to avoid repeated restart loops
  • Acts as a last-resort recovery mechanism

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Detect GPIO state changes and publish events via MQTT while maintaining a durable local event log.

docs.title: Execution
docs.text:
• Script: /opt/poopi/app/chkgpio.py
  • Type: Continuous loop (systemd-managed)
  • Poll interval: 50 ms (~20 scans/sec)

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Monitors GPIO continuously
  • Triggers only on state change (edge-based, not periodic)
  • Publishes online status on startup
  • Publishes offline status via MQTT Last Will if service stops unexpectedly

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
• GPIO pins (BCM mode):
  • 17 → surge-pump-cycle
  • 22 → surge-ops-float
  • 5 → surge-override-float
  • 27 → surge-hwa
  • 26 → effluent-pump-cycle
  • 20 → effluent-ops-float
  • 19 → effluent-override-float
  • 21 → effluent-hwa

docs.title: Processing
docs.text:
• Compare current GPIO state against last known state
  • Trigger event only on change
  • Apply logical inversion for configured pins
  • Convert to normalized binary output (0/1)

docs.title: MQTT (Primary Data Path)
docs.text:
• Broker: io.adafruit.com:1883

  • Topic format: 

    zmcnair/feeds/bkh.<label>

  • Example: 

    zmcnair/feeds/bkh.surge-ops-float

  • Payload: 

    0 or 1

  • Publish behavior:

  • Event-driven (on change only)
  • Non-blocking (background MQTT loop)

docs.title: MQTT (Status Topic)
docs.text:
• Topic: 

    zmcnair/feeds/bkh.chkgpio-status

  • Payloads:

  • 1 → running

  • 0 → offline (Last Will)

  • Retained: Yes

docs.title: File Logging (Local)
docs.text:
• Path: 

    /opt/poopi/app/gpio_events.log

  • Format: 

    timestamp,pin,label,state

  • Example: 

    2026-04-20T15:02:11.123456+00:00,22,surge-ops-float,1

  • Behavior:

  • Append-only

  • Immediate flush + fsync (write-through, no buffering loss)

  • Startup entry: 

    timestamp,SYSTEM,chkgpio_start,1

docs.title: Stdout (journald)
docs.text:
• Mirrors each event line: 

    timestamp,pin,label,state

  • View: 

    journalctl -u chkgpio.service

docs.title: Timing Characteristics
docs.text:
• ~50 ms detection resolution
  • No periodic heartbeat per signal
  • Output occurs only on transitions
  • Suitable for precise event sequencing (float changes, pump cycles)

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Acts as the primary event source for downstream systems (MQTT → processing → Grafana)
  • Downstream consumers must handle:
  • State persistence
  • Missed events if disconnected

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Generate a periodic snapshot of all GPIO states as a single JSON object for downstream consumers that require full-state visibility instead of edge-triggered events.

docs.title: Execution
docs.text:
• Script: /opt/poopi/app/gpio_heartbeat.sh
  • Type: Continuous loop (systemd-managed)
  • Interval: 5 seconds

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Captures full GPIO state at a fixed interval
  • Writes snapshot atomically (temp file → rename)
  • Independent of state changes (runs regardless of activity)

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
• GPIO pins (BCM):
  • 17
  • 22
  • 5
  • 27
  • 26
  • 20
  • 19

  • 21

  • Read method:

  • pinctrl get <pin>
  • Extracts hi / lo and converts to:
    • 1 = high
    • 0 = low

docs.title: Processing
docs.text:
• Poll all pins every 5 seconds
  • Normalize values to binary (0/1)
  • Build a single JSON object containing:
  • Timestamp (UTC)
  • Current state of all pins
  • Write snapshot using atomic file replacement:
  • Write to temp file
  • Move to final path

docs.title: File (Primary Output)
docs.text:
• Path: 

    /run/gpio_snapshot.json

  • Write method:

  • Atomic (.tmp → rename)

  • Prevents partial reads by consumers

  • Format: 

    {
  "ts": "ISO8601",
  "gpio17": 0,
  "gpio22": 1,
  "gpio5": 0,
  "gpio27": 0,
  "gpio26": 1,
  "gpio20": 1,
  "gpio19": 0,
  "gpio21": 0
}

  • Timestamp format:

  • UTC (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ)

docs.title: Timing Characteristics
docs.text:
• Fixed interval: every 5 seconds
  • Not event-driven
  • Provides state continuity even if no changes occur
  • Suitable for:
  • Polling systems
  • Recovery after missed events
  • Cross-checking event-based logs

docs.title: Relationship to chkgpio.service
docs.text:
• chkgpio.service → event-based (edge detection)
  • gpio-heartbeat.service → state-based (full snapshot)

Together they provide: - High-resolution event tracking - Periodic full-state verification

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• No MQTT output; file-based only
  • Consumers must read /run/gpio_snapshot.json
  • /run is typically tmpfs (non-persistent across reboot)

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Measure septic tank level using a 4–20 mA sensor via ADS1115 and publish calibrated depth readings to MQTT.

docs.title: Execution
docs.text:
• Script: /opt/poopi/app/level-gem2.py
  • Type: Continuous loop (systemd-managed)
  • Interval: 1 second

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Reads analog signal from ADS1115
  • Converts to voltage → current (mA) → depth (inches)
  • Publishes continuously (not event-driven)
  • Skips publish if sensor current is outside expected range (3–22 mA)

docs.title: Hardware
docs.text:
• ADC: ADS1115 (I2C, address 0x48)
  • Shunt resistor: 150 Ω
  • Gain setting: ±4.096 V

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
• Analog signal from 4–20 mA level sensor
  • I2C read via smbus2

docs.title: Processing
docs.text:
• Convert raw ADC value → voltage
  • Convert voltage → current (mA)
  • Apply calibration:
  • MA_DRY = 3.8
  • INCHES_PER_MA = 5.01
  • Calculate:
  • Inches above sensor
  • Total depth (adds fixed hang height)

docs.title: Derived Values
docs.text:
• Above sensor: 

    (mA - MA_DRY) * INCHES_PER_MA

  • Total depth: 

    above_sensor + HANG_HEIGHT

  • Clamp:

  • Negative depth values forced to 0

  • Fault handling:

  • Ignore readings outside 3–22 mA

docs.title: MQTT (Primary Output)
docs.text:
• Broker: localhost:1883

  • Topic: 

    sensors/septic/level

  • Publish rate:

  • Every 1 second (continuous stream)

  • Payload: 

    {
  "volts": float,
  "ma": float,
  "above_sensor_in": float,
  "total_depth_in": float
}

  • Example: 

    {
  "volts": 1.503,
  "ma": 10.02,
  "above_sensor_in": 31.15,
  "total_depth_in": 36.65
}

docs.title: Stdout (journald)
docs.text:
• Prints formatted readings every cycle: 

    volts | mA | inches above sensor | total depth

  • Fault condition: 

    Sensor current out of expected range

  • View: 

    journalctl -u level-gem.service

docs.title: Timing Characteristics
docs.text:
• Fixed interval: 1 second
  • Continuous publishing (not event-based)
  • Provides smooth time-series data for Grafana
  • Dependent on ADC read + small delay (~100 ms conversion + 1 s sleep)

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Acts as the continuous analog telemetry source for tank level
  • Complements:
  • chkgpio.service (event-based discrete states)
  • gpio-heartbeat.service (periodic full-state snapshot)
  • Uses local MQTT broker, likely feeding:
  • Node-RED
  • InfluxDB
  • Grafana
